evals.prompt: I need a health component for my enemies in Unity. It should track current and max health, let things take damage, die when health hits zero, and fire C# events when health changes or the enemy dies. Also needs a way to heal. Put it in the Combat namespace.
evals.expected_output: A single well-structured MonoBehaviour following code-standards conventions from core-conventions.md
expectations: Triages as Quick mode
Loads reference-routing.md to identify needed code-standards
Loads core-conventions.md from unity-standards for code patterns, naming, and attributes
Creates exactly one .cs file
Class is sealed and inherits MonoBehaviour
Uses namespace containing Combat
Health fields use [SerializeField] private with read-only properties (code-standards serialization pattern)
Has C# events (event Action) for health change and death (code-standards event pattern)
TakeDamage and Heal methods with guard clauses for invalid input (code-standards null safety pattern)
Follows _camelCase for private fields, PascalCase for methods (code-standards naming)
No GetComponent in Update or hot path
No TODO, stubs, or placeholder code
lsp_diagnostics is called on the file

evals.prompt: Build a wave-based enemy spawner system for my Unity game. I need ScriptableObject configs for each wave (enemy prefab, count, spawn interval, delay before wave), a spawner MonoBehaviour that reads the configs and spawns enemies in sequence, and events for wave start/complete/all waves done. The project uses VContainer for DI and the namespace is MyGame.Combat.
evals.expected_output: 3-5 well-structured C# files created in dependency order following code-standards architecture and event patterns
expectations: Triages as Deep mode
Loads reference-routing.md to identify needed code-standards
Loads architecture-systems.md from unity-standards for DI and event patterns
Loads core-conventions.md from unity-standards for code patterns and naming
Creates WaveConfig ScriptableObject with CreateAssetMenu (code-standards SO pattern)
Creates a spawner MonoBehaviour that is sealed (code-standards access modifier pattern)
Fires C# events for wave lifecycle — start, complete, all done (code-standards event pattern)
Uses VContainer [Inject] pattern for dependencies in consumer MonoBehaviours (code-standards DI pattern)
Files created in dependency order: data/interfaces first, then logic, then wiring
Namespace is MyGame.Combat on all files (code-standards namespace convention)
All serialized fields use [SerializeField] private with read-only properties
No stubs or incomplete wiring
lsp_diagnostics called on all files

evals.prompt: Create an async save system for my Unity game. I need a SaveData class with player stats (health, position, level, play time), a SaveService that writes/reads JSON to Application.persistentDataPath using async file I/O with proper cancellation, and error handling for missing/corrupted files. The project uses UniTask. Namespace is MyGame.Core.
evals.expected_output: 2-3 well-structured C# files following code-standards async, error handling, and serialization patterns
expectations: Triages as Deep mode
Loads reference-routing.md to identify needed code-standards
Loads lifecycle-async-errors.md from unity-standards for async and error handling patterns
Loads performance-data.md from unity-standards for serialization patterns
Creates SaveData class with proper serialization attributes (code-standards serialization pattern)
Creates SaveService with async methods using UniTask — matches project convention (code-standards async guidance)
All async methods accept CancellationToken parameter (code-standards cancellation pattern)
Uses try/catch with specific exception types, not bare catch (code-standards error handling)
Validates deserialized data — clamps values to valid ranges (code-standards save data security)
Uses Application.persistentDataPath for file paths (code-standards security rule)
Follows _camelCase for private fields, PascalCase for methods (code-standards naming)
No TODO, stubs, or placeholder code
lsp_diagnostics called on all files
